summary: End-to-end backend + frontend testing of EuropaDrop v1.1 features (rename, product sync-status tabs, payment tracking, WooCommerce webhook, CRON scheduler, DeepSeek AI). 15/15 NEW backend tests PASS. 24/25 regression tests PASS (1 non-idempotent test failure due to stale data — not a code bug). All requested frontend flows verified: EUROPADROP branding in sidebar, Catalogue 3-tab filter (Tous/Importés/Publiés with counts), product drawer AI buttons (Traduire+SEO — properly call DeepSeek and 500 due to invalid key as expected), Orders Paiement column + payment section in drawer with successful save toast, Automatisations page shows scheduler (2 jobs), webhook URL/secret + run-now buttons.

minor.issue: test_bulk_fulfill_and_tracking in regression suite is not idempotent - once all pending orders are fulfilled the test fails. Endpoint itself works. Recommend re-seeding orders or creating a fresh pending order inside the test.

Seeded orders MB-2026-0001..0004 do NOT include paymentStatus/paymentMethod/paymentReference fields (contrary to review request stating they should be pre-populated with Carte Visa/PayPal/SEPA/unpaid). Fields become available only after PUT /api/orders/{id}/payment. UI still renders correctly (shows dash + 'En attente' badge for missing paymentStatus).

action_items: OPTIONAL: Update /app/backend/seed.py to seed paymentStatus/paymentMethod on the 4 sample orders (MB-2026-0001..0003 paid with Carte Visa/PayPal/SEPA, 0004 unpaid) per PRD. Currently seeded orders lack these fields until manually updated via PUT.
OPTIONAL: Make backend_test.py::test_bulk_fulfill_and_tracking idempotent (create a fresh pending order inside the test rather than relying on seed state).

NOTE: DeepSeek API key sk-5c5375...33e6 returns 401 'invalid api key'. This is a user-provided-key issue — code path is verified working end-to-end (httpx client reaches api.deepseek.com and surfaces the 401 as 500 to caller).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py has grown to 1114 lines — recommend splitting into routers/ (auth, suppliers, products, orders, wc, ai, scheduler, webhooks).

Webhook signature verification is permissive: returns True when secret missing OR signature header missing (line 993). Acceptable for dev but should be tightened before production (require secret+signature match in prod mode).
deepseek.py `translate_to_french` is called sequentially in bulk-translate loop — consider asyncio.gather for parallelism.
scheduler.py imports server functions inside job body (circular-import workaround) — acceptable but note fragility.
products_stats does not honor category/isActive filters — currently returns global counts only. Ok for MVP but doc it.
Payment PUT endpoint does not validate paymentStatus enum values — a client could send arbitrary strings. Recommend Literal type or enum validation.

success_rate.backend: 97.5% (39/40 total: 15/15 new + 24/25 regression, 1 pre-existing stale-data test issue)

seed_data_creation: Test suite creates 1 WooCommerce webhook order (WP-987654) which persists in DB. Also updates payment status on MB-2026-0001 to paid Carte Visa via PUT /api/orders/{id}/payment. Safe to re-run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All v1.1 features working. Backend suite at /app/backend/tests/ — run `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. DeepSeek endpoints intentionally 500 due to invalid user key sk-5c5375...33e6 (code path verified). WooCommerce webhook signature check is bypassed in dev when header missing. Payment fields added to Order model. New endpoints all documented in server.py (payment, ai, webhooks, scheduler). If MB-2026-0001..0004 need seeded payment fields, update seed.py per action_items.
rca of the issue: Only failure (test_bulk_fulfill_and_tracking) — RCA: The test filters for status=pending orders and fails when no pending orders exist. Previous iteration_1 run consumed the pending orders (MB-2026-0001, 0004) by fulfilling them. Reproduction: run the full backend_test.py twice — second run fails. Mitigation: modify the test to POST a fresh order via /api/orders before bulk-fulfill, or reset seed data. Not a code bug in the /api/orders/bulk-fulfill endpoint itself.
